description:
You are a QA engineer preparing a quick smoke-test for an upcoming release.  
    A JSON file that lists every micro-service and its semantic version is
    already present on the system at

        /home/user/data/release_config.json

    Your goal: write a single terminal command that

    1. Reads that JSON file with jq.  
    2. Counts how many service objects have a **version** field that does NOT
       match the semantic-version pattern  <vMAJOR.MINOR.PATCH>  
       (i.e. the value must start with a literal “v”, followed by three
       dot-separated positive integers such as  v2.4.19).  
    3. Writes the result to the file

        /home/user/logs/version_mismatch.log

       in exactly the following one-line format (no extra spaces or newlines):

           non_conforming: N

       …where N is the integer count returned by your jq expression.

    Notes & requirements:

    • The directory  /home/user/logs  may not exist; create it if necessary.  
    • Do **everything** (the jq evaluation, any directory creation, and the
      log-file write) in a single compound command line – shell operators such
      as &&, ||, ;, pipes, subshells and redirects are all allowed, but the
      entire solution must be executed as one line that can be copied/pasted
      into the terminal.  
    • Do not output anything to stdout or create any other files.  
    • When you are finished, the automated test will inspect
      /home/user/logs/version_mismatch.log and verify that it contains exactly
      the string “non_conforming: N” followed by a single newline character.  

    (You do not need sudo for any step.)
truth:
Initial filesystem state:

    1. Directory /home/user/data exists (0755).
    2. File /home/user/data/release_config.json exists (0644) with the exact
       contents:

[
  { "service": "auth",    "version": "v1.2.0", "port": 8000 },
  { "service": "billing", "version": "1.0.0",  "port": 8010 },
  { "service": "search",  "version": "v2.0.1", "port": 8020 },
  { "service": "legacy",  "version": "v3.0",   "port": 8030 },
  { "service": "reports", "version": "v4.1.0", "port": 8040 }
]

       (JSON is compact-formatted except for pretty-printing whitespace shown
       above; the test harness compares structurally parsed JSON.)

    Expected final state after correct command execution:

    • A directory  /home/user/logs  (0755) exists.  
    • A file      /home/user/logs/version_mismatch.log  (0644) exists and
      contains exactly:

non_conforming: 2\n

      (That is the ASCII text “non_conforming: 2” followed by a single newline
       character, with the digit 2 because two objects do not match the regex:
       “billing” and “legacy”.)
name: task_000000_d5a93e07
